known_issues: Local test in this distrobox reached shader compilation and then closed the window after brief launch.
A prior local run reached gameplay but ran too fast, which suggests a frame pacing or refresh-rate issue still needs a source-backed fix.
Valve marks gamescope frame limiter support as unavailable for this title, so do not use gamescope frame limiting as the first fix path.
local_notes: Do not keep experimental Steam launch options on this app unless they are recorded here.
Historical ProtonDB reports mention PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1, but those reports are old and should not be treated as the current default without retesting.
No per-game Steam LaunchOptions were found locally when checked on 2026-05-03.

source_notes.note: ProtonDB detailed community reports include historical Large Address Aware and save/crash notes.
SteamDB records Steam Deck Verified, SteamOS compatible, recommended runtime proton-stable, and gamescope frame limiter unsupported.
Official Steam Deck compatibility endpoint currently reports Deck Verified and SteamOS Playable for this app.
Konami/Steam news states the collection is fully playable on Steam Deck.

known_issues: Native launch directly under the distrobox/Hyprland/Xwayland dual-monitor layout logs Desktop is 0 x 0 @ 0 Hz and exits or leaves a hidden CPU-burning process.
The failure is local display detection, not Proton compatibility. OpenGL and Vulkan are visible, and gamescope gives Unity a valid 2560x1440 display.
local_notes: SteamDB records the Linux launch option as artofrally.x64.
No per-game Steam LaunchOptions were found locally when checked on 2026-05-03.
A direct distrobox test with gamescope reached a visible art of rally window and loaded into the game after setting SteamAppId=550320 and SteamGameId=550320.

The direct test bypasses Steam's launch wrapper and Steam Input, so controller detection must be validated from a normal Steam launch with the gamescope launch option, not from the direct binary test.
If the game appears to do nothing, check for a stale artofrally.x64 process. Unity single-instance mode blocks relaunches while the hidden process remains.

source_notes.note: Steam store lists SteamOS/Linux support and Linux system requirements.
SteamDB records Windows, macOS, and Linux support; Steam Deck Verified; recommended runtime native; and Linux executable artofrally.x64.
ProtonDB summary is Platinum, but native remains the preferred first path because Valve recommends native.

known_issues: Local test needed the distrobox Steam NVIDIA 32-bit library fix before 32-bit Proton/DXVK games launched reliably.
local_notes: Local Sonic Adventure DX test worked after the Steam runtime library path fix and setting the game config to windowed mode.

source_notes.note: Use ProtonDB as the compatibility baseline, then keep the local windowed-mode note because it is specific to this setup.
